cards.question: Define isotopes.
cards.answer: Atoms of the same element with the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons.
cards.explanation: Both halves needed. 'Same element, different mass number' also scores.

cards.question: Define relative atomic mass (Ar).
cards.answer: The weighted mean mass of an atom of an element compared with 1/12 the mass of an atom of carbon-12.
markPoints: weighted mean mass of an atom
cards.explanation: 'Weighted mean' and the carbon-12 reference are both required for full marks.

cards.question: Define relative isotopic mass.
cards.answer: The mass of an atom of a particular isotope compared with 1/12 the mass of an atom of carbon-12.
markPoints: mass of one isotope vs 1/12 mass of carbon-12
cards.explanation: Isotopic mass refers to ONE isotope; atomic mass is the weighted mean of all of them.

cards.question: Chlorine has two isotopes: ³⁵Cl (75.0%) and ³⁷Cl (25.0%). Calculate the relative atomic mass of chlorine to 3 significant figures.
cards.explanation: Multiply each isotopic mass by its % abundance, sum, divide by 100 (total abundance).

cards.question: A mass spectrum of element X shows peaks at m/z = 63 (69.2%) and m/z = 65 (30.8%). Calculate the relative atomic mass of X to 1 decimal place and identify X.
cards.explanation: Same weighted-mean method, then match Ar to the periodic table (63.6 ≈ Cu).

cards.question: Explain why isotopes of the same element have identical chemical properties.
cards.answer: They have the same number of electrons / the same electron configuration, and chemical reactions involve electrons. The different number of neutrons does not affect chemical behaviour.
cards.explanation: Chemistry = electrons. Neutrons only change mass (physical properties like density/rate of diffusion).
